Review Text:
Beware of Fuel Meter Scams at This Petrol Pump
I strongly advise everyone to stay alert while refueling at this petrol pump. There have been multiple instances where customers were allegedly scammed through meter manipulation.
Always make sure to stand near the meter and verify that the reading is set to zero before the attendant starts filling petrol. Do not allow them to begin fueling while you are away from the meter. Once the process starts without showing zero, the reading may already include a previous amount, which means you could end up paying for fuel you did not receive.
The staff here seem very skilled at distracting customers during the process, which makes it easier for such scams to happen. Stay attentive, watch the meter carefully, and insist on seeing the zero reading clearly before the pump is started.

Beware of Fuel Meter Scams at This Petrol Pump
I strongly advise everyone to stay alert while refueling at this petrol pump. There have been multiple instances where customers were allegedly scammed through meter manipulation.
Always make sure to stand near the meter and verify that the reading is set to zero before the attendant starts filling petrol. Do not allow them to begin fueling while you are away from the meter. Once the process starts without showing zero, the reading may already include a previous amount, which means you could end up paying for fuel you did not receive.
The staff here seem very skilled at distracting customers during the process, which makes it easier for such scams to happen. Stay attentive, watch the meter carefully, and insist on seeing the zero reading clearly before the pump is started.

Review Text:
Beware of Fuel Meter Scams at This Petrol Pump
I strongly advise everyone to stay alert while refueling at this petrol pump. There have been multiple instances where customers were allegedly scammed through meter manipulation.
Always make sure to stand near the meter and verify that the reading is set to zero before the attendant starts filling petrol. Do not allow them to begin fueling while you are away from the meter. Once the process starts without showing zero, the reading may already include a previous amount, which means you could end up paying for fuel you did not receive.
The staff here seem very skilled at distracting customers during the process, which makes it easier for such scams to happen. Stay attentive, watch the meter carefully, and insist on seeing the zero reading clearly before the pump is started.

Review Text:
### Mahanagar Gas CNG Station, Aarey Road, Goregaon East: A Detailed Review

The Mahanagar Gas CNG Station on Aarey Road, Goregaon East, is a well-equipped fuel station offering CNG, petrol, and diesel, along with additional services like a free air pump, a paid nitrogen pump, and lube oils. While the station itself is functional and provides all essential services, accessing it presents significant challenges that affect the overall convenience and safety.

### Accessibility Issues
Reaching the Mahanagar Gas CNG Station is notably difficult due to its location relative to the Western Express Highway (WEH). If you are approaching from the north side, there is no direct left turn into Aarey Road from the highway. Instead, drivers must continue past the Oberoi Mall flyover, take a service lane, and navigate back to reach the station. This detour can be time-consuming, often taking no less than 20 minutes due to traffic and multiple signals.

Similarly, for those coming from the south side, the journey is equally cumbersome. Drivers need to pass under the Oberoi Mall flyover and take a U-turn, adding to the travel time and causing delays. The combination of traffic signals and the busy nature of the area around the flyover exacerbates the problem, making what should be a quick stop a prolonged ordeal.

### Safety Concerns
The absence of a direct left cut from the northbound lane of WEH also raises serious safety concerns. Many drivers, in an attempt to save time, take dangerous shortcuts by crossing into the wrong lane after descending from the Oberoi Mall flyover to make an illegal U-turn. This risky maneuver poses a significant hazard, increasing the likelihood of accidents with vehicles traveling on the service lane from the north. The situation highlights a critical need for better traffic management and enforcement to prevent these unsafe practices.

### Fuel Station Services
Once you manage to reach the Mahanagar Gas CNG Station, the service is generally satisfactory. The station offers a full range of fuels—CNG, petrol, and diesel—catering to a variety of vehicles. Additionally, there is a free air pump available for tire inflation, along with a paid nitrogen pump for those who prefer it. Lube oils are also available, making it a convenient one-stop shop for various automotive needs.

### Conclusion
The Mahanagar Gas CNG Station on Aarey Road is a well-equipped fuel stop with all the essential services you would expect, including CNG, petrol, diesel, air, nitrogen, and lube oils. However, the major drawback is the challenging and time-consuming access route, whether you are approaching from the north or south on WEH. The safety risks posed by drivers taking illegal shortcuts to reach the station further complicate the situation. While the station itself is adequate, the accessibility and safety issues significantly impact the overall convenience and could benefit from better traffic management solutions.
All Issue Tags: Safety Concern, Air, Tyre & Nitrogen, Traffic & Accessibility

The fuel quality was good, and the prices were reasonable, making it a great option for those looking for affordable fuel options. The pump also had a small convenience store that offered snacks and beverages, which was quite convenient.

Station Address: 100, Bhulabhai Desai Marg, Breach Candy, Cumballa Hill, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400026
Review Text:
Concerns Regarding Night Staff Conduct at Petrol Pump
Here's a summary of your concerns and the areas where improvement is needed:
* Fueling and Payments: The primary duties of the night staff are to dispense fuel and process payments. This should be their focus.
* Windshield Wiper Fluid: It's appropriate for staff to ask if a customer needs their windshield wiper fluid topped up and to do so if requested.
* Engine Oil Checks (Critical Issue):
* Inappropriate Timing: Checking engine oil in front of the car owner (the "Boss") is problematic, especially when the car has just been driven. When a car is running, or has just been turned off after running, the engine oil is circulating and hasn't had time to settle back into the oil pan. This will lead to an inaccurate (lower) reading on the dipstick.
* Misleading Information: Telling a car owner in front of their driver that the vehicle is low on oil, based on an inaccurate reading, can lead to misunderstandings, anger the owner, and potentially create distrust in the driver's competency.
* Professionalism: It's unprofessional to suggest a vehicle has a problem without proper assessment, especially in a public setting and in front of an employer.
Training Requirements for Staff
It's clear that the night staff needs proper training on how to accurately check engine oil levels. This training should cover:
* When to Check: Emphasize that engine oil should be checked when the engine is cold, or at least after it has been turned off for a sufficient period (e.g., 5-10 minutes) to allow the oil to drain back into the pan.
* How to Check: Correct demonstration of using the dipstick, wiping it clean, reinserting it fully, and reading the level accurately.
* Customer Interaction: Instruct staff on appropriate ways to offer services without making unsolicited and potentially misleading claims about vehicle condition, especially concerning critical components like engine oil, in front of vehicle owners. They should be polite and focused on their core duties.
Thank you again for highlighting these issues. We will address them with the night staff to ensure better service and avoid such situations in the future.
All Issue Tags: Staff Behaviour, Staff Helpfulness, Operating Hours

Garuda Petrol and CNG Station stands out as a reliable and convenient option for those seeking a hassle-free CNG refueling experience.  While it offers petrol services as well, its strength undeniably lies in its CNG infrastructure and the overall positive experience it provides to its CNG clientele.  This review will delve into the various aspects of the station, highlighting its strengths and areas for potential improvement.

One of the most immediately noticeable advantages is the station's well-maintained CNG dispensing system.  The equipment appears modern and efficient, minimizing wait times.  During my several visits, I rarely encountered long queues, even during peak hours. This efficiency speaks volumes about the station's capacity and its effective management of customer flow. The dispensing process itself is smooth and straightforward, with clear instructions and helpful attendants readily available to assist if needed.  The digital displays are easy to read, clearly indicating the amount of CNG dispensed and the total cost.  This transparency builds trust and ensures accurate billing.

Beyond the technical aspects, the station's overall cleanliness and organization contribute significantly to the positive experience.  The premises are well-lit and maintained, free from litter and debris.  The designated parking areas are spacious enough to accommodate a variety of vehicles, making maneuvering easy even for larger CNG-powered vehicles.  This attention to detail creates a pleasant and comfortable environment for customers, a stark contrast to some other stations where disorganization and uncleanliness can be a major drawback.

The staff at Garuda Petrol and CNG Station deserve special commendation.  They are consistently polite, helpful, and efficient.  Their willingness to assist customers with any queries or issues, whether it's regarding the refueling process or general inquiries, is commendable.  This friendly and professional demeanor significantly enhances the overall customer experience, making the refueling stop a less stressful and more pleasant part of the journey.  Their knowledge of the CNG system is also evident, allowing them to quickly address any concerns or troubleshoot minor issues.

Furthermore, the station's location plays a crucial role in its convenience.  Its strategic placement [mention specific location details if known, e.g., near a major highway, in a densely populated area] makes it easily accessible for a large number of CNG vehicle owners.  This accessibility is a significant factor for many drivers, as finding reliable CNG stations can sometimes be a challenge.  The availability of other amenities nearby, such as restrooms or convenience stores [mention if applicable], further enhances the overall convenience factor.

However, no establishment is without room for improvement. While the wait times are generally short, occasional peak periods might still lead to slightly longer queues.  Implementing a queuing system or providing real-time queue information through an app or website could further optimize the customer experience during these busy times.  Additionally, while the staff is generally excellent, providing additional training on advanced troubleshooting techniques could further enhance their capabilities and ensure even faster resolution of any unforeseen issues.

In conclusion, Garuda Petrol and CNG Station provides a consistently positive CNG refueling experience.  Its efficient dispensing system, clean premises, helpful staff, and convenient location make it a top choice for CNG vehicle owners.  While minor improvements could further enhance the experience, the overall quality of service and the focus on customer satisfaction make it a highly recommended refueling stop.  The station's commitment to providing a smooth and efficient CNG refueling experience sets it apart from many others, making it a valuable asset for the CNG-powered vehicle community.

Review Text:
Top-Notch Service and Reliability: Mahanagar Gas CNG and Petrol Station

I've been a regular patron of Mahanagar Gas CNG and Petrol Station for quite some time now, and I must say, their service and reliability never cease to impress me. This station has truly set the standard for what a trusted petrol pump should be.

From the moment you pull up to the station, you're greeted by a well-organized setup and a team of efficient staff members. The entire process, whether you're filling up with CNG or petrol, is streamlined and hassle-free. It's evident that the staff takes pride in their work, as they always go above and beyond to ensure that customers are attended to promptly and with a smile.

One aspect that sets Mahanagar Gas apart is their unwavering commitment to quality. I've always found their fuel to be of the highest standard, giving me peace of mind every time I fill up. The station is well-maintained, and I appreciate the attention to cleanliness and safety protocols.

Moreover, I must commend the staff for their professionalism and knowledge. Whether I have questions about fuel options or need assistance with my vehicle, they're always willing to lend a helping hand. It's clear that customer satisfaction is a top priority here, and it shows in every interaction.

In conclusion, Mahanagar Gas CNG and Petrol Station is more than just a fueling station – it's a beacon of excellence in the industry. With excellent service, a team of trusted professionals, and a well-organized facility, this station has earned my unwavering trust and loyalty. I highly recommend Mahanagar Gas to anyone in need of fueling services.

Station Address: Ground Floor, Jerbai Wadia Rd, opposite TATA MEMORIAL HOSPITAL, Parel East, Parel, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400012
Review Text:
We had a subpar experience at a gas station not too long ago, which made us dissatisfied and doubt the employees' honesty.

What was supposed to be a standard fuel stop became a concerning encounter that revealed dishonest behaviour and attempted to mislead clients.

We noted the employees' glaring lack of efficiency and excitement when we pulled up to the pump. Clearly, they had a lazy attitude and were not interested in doing the required service.

It soon became evident—to our complete dismay—that the employees were using dishonest tactics to defraud clients.

We saw them purposefully dispense less fuel than what was indicated by the meter, shortchanging others who weren't expecting it. In addition to betraying customer confidence, this flagrant dishonesty casts considerable doubt on the establishment's dedication to doing business morally and ethically.

The events that followed were much more upsetting. When a consumer raised legitimate concerns about the questionable fuel measurement, the employees used a vile strategy.

Rather than professionally confronting the matter, they assembled as a group, resembling a mob, and seemed to be trying to threaten the consumer. Such conduct is wholly inappropriate in any customer service environment, in addition to being unprofessional.

The employees' behaviour at this gas station was quite concerning, in addition to being immoral. Seeing those supposed to be servicing consumers using dishonesty and threats is unfortunate. This episode casts considerable questions on the management's monitoring and dedication to promoting an open and honest culture.

We strongly encourage people to proceed cautiously if they plan to refuel here.
All Issue Tags: Meter Tampering & Fraud, Staff Behaviour, Fuel Short Filling

Review Text:
Please be aware of scammers. On April 26th at around 6:30 AM, two young men attempted to scam me by claiming that the air pressure in my back tire was low, around 9 PSI. Since I was with my infant in the car, I decided to check the back tire. They removed the tire and pretended to search for a puncture. However, there was no leakage, and one of them marked a spot, saying it was the puncture point, and even attempted to puncture the tire.

I insisted they show me the leak with a solution, but there was no puncture. I realized it was a scam. Please be cautious and avoid getting air or nitrogen filled by similar individuals. They wasted my time and could have easily damaged my tire with their unnecessary actions. Afterward, I drove 120 km and checked the air pressure again at another petrol station; the tire pressure hadn’t dropped at all.

Stay alert to protect yourself from scammers. The photographs included are from my dashcam.
All Issue Tags: Air, Tyre & Nitrogen, Meter Tampering & Fraud, Safety Concern

Review Text:
🚨 Beware of Cheating at this Petrol Pump! 🚨

I had a very bad experience here with DEF fluid filling. First of all, when I asked one person if DEF was available, he confidently said “No”. Then another person came and said “Yes” – clearly trying to confuse customers.

I purchased 10 liters DEF for ₹850. My car’s DEF tank capacity is 20 liters, and it was already at 54% (Approximately 10 litres). So, after adding 10 liters, it should have reached 100% full.

But after they “filled” it, the meter showed only 71%. That means they filled just 4 liters instead of 10 liters. When I questioned them, they gave silly excuses like “The remaining fluid in the can won’t come out”. They also did not even use a proper funnel – just pushed a pipe inside carelessly.

When I checked the can, more than 5 liters were still left inside, but the staff shamelessly claimed it was “finished”. I’ve also added pictures of Car DEF level and remaining volume of DEF in Can. This is nothing but open cheating and fraud with customers.

1)  They charge for 10 liters but fill less.
2)  Staff keep confusing answers to trap customers.
3)  No proper equipment or honesty in service.

⚠️ My advice: Avoid this petrol pump at all costs. They are looting people in the name of DEF fluid. Please always come out of your vehicle and check carefully while refueling here. This pump cannot be trusted.

Details for Reference (if required):
• Vehicle No.: MH47BE0360
• Date & Time of Purchase: 29 August 2025, ~7:30 PM

Review Text: It’s a blatant scam! It’s happened with us more than once now at this pump. Our car will not show even a single km rise in the fuel tank indicator. Station Address: Western Express Hwy, near Domestic Airport, Navpada, Vile Parle East, Vile Parle, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400099
Review Text:
Located strategically near the Vile Parle airport area, this Indian Oil outlet is a frequent stop for many travelers and locals alike. While there are several things they get right, there are a few persistent operational issues that prevent it from being a top-tier station.

The Positives: Hospitality and Maintenance
The most impressive aspect of this pump is the conduct of the staff. In a city where fuel station employees are often rushed and dismissive, the team here stands out. They are polite, professional, and well-coordinated in managing the constant flow of vehicles, especially during peak airport hours.
Another huge benefit is the Free Nitrogen Air facility.
Most stations in Mumbai either have broken air machines or charge extra for Nitrogen.
Here, the machine is usually functional, and the service is free, which is a massive plus for vehicle maintenance and tire longevity.
The fuel quality also seems consistent, and I’ve never had an issue with the "zero-check" or the accuracy of the dispensing units.

The Major Drawback: Payment & Connectivity
The reason for my 3-star rating boils down to the frequent and frustrating payment failures. In a digital-first economy, being told "the server is down" is a significant inconvenience.

Digital Payment Reliability: On several visits, they have refused to accept Credit/Debit cards or UPI (Google Pay/PhonePe), citing server issues or machine connectivity problems.

The Cash Scramble: Since this pump is near the airport, many customers are on their way to a flight or have just landed. Not everyone carries enough cash for a full tank of fuel. Being told at the last minute that online payments aren't working creates unnecessary stress and delays.

Areas for Improvement
While the staff is doing their best, the management needs to prioritize their IT infrastructure.

Backup Systems: They should invest in a more stable internet connection or have multiple POS (Point of Sale) machines from different banks to ensure card payments always go through.

Advance Warning: If the server is truly down, there should be a prominent sign at the entrance so customers aren't caught off guard after the fuel has already been pumped.

Final Verdict
Go here for the polite service and the free Nitrogen, but always carry cash just in case. It’s a reliable station for fuel quality, but the recurring "server down" excuse for digital payments is a hurdle that needs to be fixed to earn back those extra stars.
All Issue Tags: Payment Methods, Waiting Time & Queue, Air, Tyre & Nitrogen

Station Address: Ground Floor, IB Patel Road, Flyover, opposite Aarey, Sonawala Industry Estate, Goregaon East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400063
Review Text:
Beware of Fuel Meter Scams at This Petrol Pump
I strongly advise everyone to stay alert while refueling at this petrol pump. There have been multiple instances where customers were allegedly scammed through meter manipulation.
Always make sure to stand near the meter and verify that the reading is set to zero before the attendant starts filling petrol. Do not allow them to begin fueling while you are away from the meter. Once the process starts without showing zero, the reading may already include a previous amount, which means you could end up paying for fuel you did not receive.
The staff here seem very skilled at distracting customers during the process, which makes it easier for such scams to happen. Stay attentive, watch the meter carefully, and insist on seeing the zero reading clearly before the pump is started.

Review Text:
### Mahanagar Gas CNG Station, Aarey Road, Goregaon East: A Detailed Review

The Mahanagar Gas CNG Station on Aarey Road, Goregaon East, is a well-equipped fuel station offering CNG, petrol, and diesel, along with additional services like a free air pump, a paid nitrogen pump, and lube oils. While the station itself is functional and provides all essential services, accessing it presents significant challenges that affect the overall convenience and safety.

### Accessibility Issues
Reaching the Mahanagar Gas CNG Station is notably difficult due to its location relative to the Western Express Highway (WEH). If you are approaching from the north side, there is no direct left turn into Aarey Road from the highway. Instead, drivers must continue past the Oberoi Mall flyover, take a service lane, and navigate back to reach the station. This detour can be time-consuming, often taking no less than 20 minutes due to traffic and multiple signals.

Similarly, for those coming from the south side, the journey is equally cumbersome. Drivers need to pass under the Oberoi Mall flyover and take a U-turn, adding to the travel time and causing delays. The combination of traffic signals and the busy nature of the area around the flyover exacerbates the problem, making what should be a quick stop a prolonged ordeal.

### Safety Concerns
The absence of a direct left cut from the northbound lane of WEH also raises serious safety concerns. Many drivers, in an attempt to save time, take dangerous shortcuts by crossing into the wrong lane after descending from the Oberoi Mall flyover to make an illegal U-turn. This risky maneuver poses a significant hazard, increasing the likelihood of accidents with vehicles traveling on the service lane from the north. The situation highlights a critical need for better traffic management and enforcement to prevent these unsafe practices.

### Fuel Station Services
Once you manage to reach the Mahanagar Gas CNG Station, the service is generally satisfactory. The station offers a full range of fuels—CNG, petrol, and diesel—catering to a variety of vehicles. Additionally, there is a free air pump available for tire inflation, along with a paid nitrogen pump for those who prefer it. Lube oils are also available, making it a convenient one-stop shop for various automotive needs.

### Conclusion
The Mahanagar Gas CNG Station on Aarey Road is a well-equipped fuel stop with all the essential services you would expect, including CNG, petrol, diesel, air, nitrogen, and lube oils. However, the major drawback is the challenging and time-consuming access route, whether you are approaching from the north or south on WEH. The safety risks posed by drivers taking illegal shortcuts to reach the station further complicate the situation. While the station itself is adequate, the accessibility and safety issues significantly impact the overall convenience and could benefit from better traffic management solutions.
All Issue Tags: Safety Concern, Air, Tyre & Nitrogen, Traffic & Accessibility

Station Address: Ground Floor, Western Express Hwy, next to Metro Mall, Food Corporation of India Warehouse, Borivali East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400066
Review Text:
A scam is happening involving so-called “scratch removal” people at this petrol pump.
A man carrying a scratch-removal kit approaches customers and tries to sell the product. Yesterday, I brought my car here right after getting it freshly painted and all scratches removed at the service centre. While I was inside, this person came to me again and insisted he could remove a scratch. I clearly told him the car was newly painted and completely scratch-free.
But the moment I stepped out to check, I was shocked to see a big scratch on my car.
I am 100% sure this was done by the same “scratch removal” team operating at this petrol pump.
Please be extremely careful while filling petrol here, or avoid this pump altogether.
There is too much traffic at this petrol pump, and there is no openness, which allows such people to do whatever they want unnoticed.
I used to be a regular customer, but after this incident, I will never fill fuel at this pump again.
It’s sad that people have no respect for someone’s emotions or the hard-earned money put into maintaining their car. All they care about is earning ₹400–₹500 through such cheap tactics.
Stay alert and protect your vehicle.

Review Text:
"I'm astonished that this HP petrol pump, the only one catering to a massive area from eastern Dahisar to north east Goregaan, gets away with providing bare-minimum facilities. It's always crowded, and the attendants must be exhausted to the point of desperation, which explains their perpetual crankiness.

Meanwhile, smaller HP petrol pumps in other areas get to flaunt fancy canopy roofs and better amenities. But not this one, which serves a huge clientele. The roof over the bike refueling area is a joke, barely covering 3-4 bikes, while 30 or more wait in line, exposed to the elements.

The car refueling area isn't much better, with a canopy that can barely fit 3-4 cars. And if you need to get out to check on something during the rain, be prepared to get soaked.

The only reason I'm giving this place two stars is because of the employees, who deserve a medal for putting up with these abysmal working conditions and still managing to do their job."

Review Text:
Top-Notch Service and Reliability: Mahanagar Gas CNG and Petrol Station

I've been a regular patron of Mahanagar Gas CNG and Petrol Station for quite some time now, and I must say, their service and reliability never cease to impress me. This station has truly set the standard for what a trusted petrol pump should be.

From the moment you pull up to the station, you're greeted by a well-organized setup and a team of efficient staff members. The entire process, whether you're filling up with CNG or petrol, is streamlined and hassle-free. It's evident that the staff takes pride in their work, as they always go above and beyond to ensure that customers are attended to promptly and with a smile.

One aspect that sets Mahanagar Gas apart is their unwavering commitment to quality. I've always found their fuel to be of the highest standard, giving me peace of mind every time I fill up. The station is well-maintained, and I appreciate the attention to cleanliness and safety protocols.

Moreover, I must commend the staff for their professionalism and knowledge. Whether I have questions about fuel options or need assistance with my vehicle, they're always willing to lend a helping hand. It's clear that customer satisfaction is a top priority here, and it shows in every interaction.

In conclusion, Mahanagar Gas CNG and Petrol Station is more than just a fueling station – it's a beacon of excellence in the industry. With excellent service, a team of trusted professionals, and a well-organized facility, this station has earned my unwavering trust and loyalty. I highly recommend Mahanagar Gas to anyone in need of fueling services.

Station Address: Aarey Milk Colony, Goregaon East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400065
Review Text:
### Mahanagar Gas CNG Station, Aarey Road, Goregaon East: A Detailed Review

The Mahanagar Gas CNG Station on Aarey Road, Goregaon East, is a well-equipped fuel station offering CNG, petrol, and diesel, along with additional services like a free air pump, a paid nitrogen pump, and lube oils. While the station itself is functional and provides all essential services, accessing it presents significant challenges that affect the overall convenience and safety.

### Accessibility Issues
Reaching the Mahanagar Gas CNG Station is notably difficult due to its location relative to the Western Express Highway (WEH). If you are approaching from the north side, there is no direct left turn into Aarey Road from the highway. Instead, drivers must continue past the Oberoi Mall flyover, take a service lane, and navigate back to reach the station. This detour can be time-consuming, often taking no less than 20 minutes due to traffic and multiple signals.

Similarly, for those coming from the south side, the journey is equally cumbersome. Drivers need to pass under the Oberoi Mall flyover and take a U-turn, adding to the travel time and causing delays. The combination of traffic signals and the busy nature of the area around the flyover exacerbates the problem, making what should be a quick stop a prolonged ordeal.

### Safety Concerns
The absence of a direct left cut from the northbound lane of WEH also raises serious safety concerns. Many drivers, in an attempt to save time, take dangerous shortcuts by crossing into the wrong lane after descending from the Oberoi Mall flyover to make an illegal U-turn. This risky maneuver poses a significant hazard, increasing the likelihood of accidents with vehicles traveling on the service lane from the north. The situation highlights a critical need for better traffic management and enforcement to prevent these unsafe practices.

### Fuel Station Services
Once you manage to reach the Mahanagar Gas CNG Station, the service is generally satisfactory. The station offers a full range of fuels—CNG, petrol, and diesel—catering to a variety of vehicles. Additionally, there is a free air pump available for tire inflation, along with a paid nitrogen pump for those who prefer it. Lube oils are also available, making it a convenient one-stop shop for various automotive needs.

### Conclusion
The Mahanagar Gas CNG Station on Aarey Road is a well-equipped fuel stop with all the essential services you would expect, including CNG, petrol, diesel, air, nitrogen, and lube oils. However, the major drawback is the challenging and time-consuming access route, whether you are approaching from the north or south on WEH. The safety risks posed by drivers taking illegal shortcuts to reach the station further complicate the situation. While the station itself is adequate, the accessibility and safety issues significantly impact the overall convenience and could benefit from better traffic management solutions.
All Issue Tags: Safety Concern, Air, Tyre & Nitrogen, Traffic & Accessibility

Cleanliness is also essential in a petrol pump. The station should be well-maintained and free from litter and debris. Customers are more likely to visit a petrol pump that is clean and well-organized.

Review Text:
Avoid for any real repair work❗️

These guys at Godrej Petrol Pump mechanic shop are still learning. They might manage basic stuff like fixing a puncture or oiling, but don’t trust them with actual repairs or part changes.
Here’s what happened with me:
I went there because my bike had a handling issue and needed a wash. The mechanic said he’d open the handle and check. When I came back later, the bike was still untouched. He asked for more time, and eventually gave it back after a diesel wash — which was badly done, with stains and mud still there. He admitted it and offered a free rewash, which was decent of him.
But when I took the test ride, the handling problem was still the same. He said “poora handle khol k grease kiya”. A week later, no improvement. I went back again, and he said “bearing change karna padega” - local bearing 300, original 650. I bought the original one myself for ₹450 and asked him to just replace it. After that, the issue was still not fixed.
This time, when I complained, he said “the bike is fine” basically gaslighting me. Finally, I took it to another mechanic a little farther away. He checked, tightened the handle axle bolt, charged ₹20… and the problem was completely gone.

Makes me wonder if the Godrej Pump guys ever even opened the handle at all.

Bottom line: they might be okay for quick fixes, but don’t give them your bike for repairs or part replacements. You’ll waste time and money.
All Issue Tags: Air, Tyre & Nitrogen, Facility Maintenance, Operating Hours
